units: m^2 (controllable area above positional replacement baseline)
definition: Per attacking, off-ball, non-GK player: controllable pitch area (pitch control x cell area, team-control weighted) minus the replacement baseline = the mean controllable area of all such player-frames whose centroid lands in the same pitch zone (x-band x y-channel). Averaged over the player's off-ball frames. Positive => makes MORE space than a typical player there. SPACE-NATIVE (m^2); NOT xT-multiplied.

occlusion.note: Per-player ESTIMATED share is reported as est_share; PFF marks ~40.9% of positions ESTIMATED tournament-wide, biased toward forwards/wingers. SAR is gated on a minimum VISIBLE-frame count.

xg_receipt.unit: team SAR (mean off-ball m^2 above replacement) vs StatsBomb xG-for per 90
xg_receipt.reading: SAR is statistically independent of chance creation: team SAR vs StatsBomb xG-for per 90 gives Spearman rho=-0.01 (95% CI -0.59 to 0.62, n=11 teams) -- a near-zero association whose CI straddles 0. That is the point: SAR is space-NATIVE openness, NOT an xG proxy. The teams that win the most open off-ball space are deep-block / counter sides with vacated pitch to spread into; the high-xG possession teams work CONGESTED final thirds and win LESS space above replacement. Correlation, not causation; more space does not buy more threat -- where you make it is the bridge (P-OBSO).
